description: A manuscript which contains the end of Deutronomy, and the haftarot (weekly readings from the Prophets) according to the old Palestinian rite. A dedication colophon at the end of Deutronomy specifies that the manuscript is donated to 'the gate of the priest' (שער הכהן), a well-known prayer site during the 10th-11th centuries. The colophon also includes the name of the dedicator, Muḥsin ha-Levi b. Yiṣḥaq known as Ḥadīd, the intermediaries - three brothers from the B. Yeshū'a family which is known from other Documents. Other parts of the same manuscript (according to additional colophons and the style) - RNL Yevr. II B 79.
description_he: טקסט פרא-ספרותי בעברית. מוחזק בהספרייה הלאומית רוסיה.
